content_performance.note: Real data from Justin's LinkedIn content analytics — as of 2026-06-09
content_performance.key_insight: 258 saves is a strong signal — educators bookmark posts for use in staff meetings. Write to be saved, not just liked.

format_learnings: BEST FORMAT: Single precise data point in the headline (β = 0.061, 3% vs 32%) — 59K impressions
Comparison format works: two bars showing different effect sizes makes the proportionality argument visual
Multi-source validation ('four independent research groups, same conclusion') builds credibility and handles objections pre-emptively
Short punchy opening under 10 words — every high-performer starts this way
Posts that educators save (258 saves) are practical, specific, and usable in their own context
Timeline/narrative posts underperform vs data-specific posts — 2K impressions vs 59K
The IS / ISN'T framing performs well — directly addresses misconceptions educators hold
post_topics_used: Social media as amplifier not originator of mental health crisis
β = 0.061 effect size — social media's impact statistically indistinguishable from noise
2012 claim vs 2008 reality — mental health acceleration preceded smartphones
Poverty as primary driver — 23,155 data points showing screen is downstream of deprivation
hooks_already_used: Social media didn't create the mental health crisis
The Villain Was Already In The Room
The Effect Size That Changes Everything
best_performing_formats: Lead with a precise surprising statistic
Comparison: two numbers side by side (32% vs 3%)
One concrete takeaway educators can use today

recent_actions.action: Post LinkedIn before 8.30am (ahead of the expected PM announcement), paste Substack draft mid-morning, Instagram at 12pm, Facebook at 6pm — and hold a follow-up post ready to publish the moment the speech lands.

Post LinkedIn before 8.30am (ahead of the expected PM speech), paste Substack draft mid-morning, Instagram at 12pm, Facebook at 6pm — and keep a same-day reaction post drafted to publish the moment Starmer confirms which of the three options he's chosen.

recent_questions.question: Will the announced ban define "safer" social media in a way that explicitly recognises supervised children's platforms — and do we want our public voice to lobby for that carve-out, or stay editorially neutral to protect Sage's trusted-educator credibility?

Does Guided Childhood want to be positioned publicly *as* a permitted "safer platform" under whatever model the PM picks, or as the neutral age-assurance/compliance layer other platforms buy in — because the content angle (and how hard I lean on "we are the safe environment") changes materially depending on that decision?

notes_to_self: 258 saves out of 2,009 engagements = 12.8% save rate. That is very high. Educators are using this content. Write to be saved, not just liked. The data-specific format is working — don't drift into narrative.
